2603.17102.title: Knowledge Localization in Mixture-of-Experts LLMs Using Cross-Lingual Inconsistency
2603.17102.paper_summary: Modern LLMs continue to exhibit significant variance in behavior across languages, such as being able to recall factual information in some languages but not others. While typically studied as a problem to be mitigated, in this work, we propose leveraging this cross-lingual inconsistency as a tool for interpretability in mixture-of-experts (MoE) LLMs. Our knowledge localization framework contrasts routing for sets of languages where the model correctly recalls information from languages where it fails. This allows us to isolate model components that play a functional role in answering about a piece of knowledge. Our method proceeds in two stages: (1) querying the model with difficult factual questions across a diverse set of languages to generate "success" and "failure" activation buckets and then (2) applying a statistical contrastive analysis to the MoE router logits to identify experts important for knowledge. To validate the necessity of this small number of experts for answering a knowledge question, we deactivate them and re-ask the question. We find that despite only deactivating about 20 out of 6000 experts, the model no longer answers correctly in over 40% of cases. Generally, this method provides a realistic and scalable knowledge localization approach to address increasingly complex LLMs.
2603.17102.paper_summary_zh: 現代的LLM在不同語言之間的行為仍然顯示出顯著的變異性，例如能夠在某些語言中回憶事實信息，而在其他語言中則無法。雖然通常將其視為需要減輕的問題，但在本研究中，我們提議利用這種跨語言的不一致性作為解釋混合專家（MoE）LLM的工具。我們的知識定位框架對比了在模型能夠正確回憶信息的語言集與其失敗的語言集的路由。這使我們能夠孤立出在回答某個知識時發揮功能的模型組件。我們的方法分為兩個階段：（1）在多樣的語言集中對模型提出困難的事實問題，以生成“成功”和“失敗”的激活桶，然後（2）對MoE路由器的logits應用統計對比分析，以識別對知識重要的專家。為了驗證這少數專家對回答知識問題的必要性，我們將其停用並重新提出問題。我們發現，儘管僅停用了約20個專家中的6000個，但模型在超過40%的情況下不再正確回答。總的來說，這種方法提供了一種現實且可擴展的知識定位方法，以應對日益複雜的LLM。
2603.17102.author: Lucas Bandarkar et.al.
2603.17102.authors: Lucas Bandarkar, Alan Ansell, Trevor Cohn
